mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical relationship with FEDFUNDS changes (Rank IC < 0.02) across all tested periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off selling and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.4pp in a single month
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns remain positive in 55% or more of instances where UNRATE increases exceed 0.4pp.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates robust consumer demand and construction activity, driving positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to produce positive 60-day forward returns more than 50% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: When the economy operates above full capacity, a contraction in industrial production indicates tightening supply chains that erode profit margins, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO growth during late-cycle expansions yields positive 20-day forward returns in over 60% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price index values increase inflation expectations, prompting markets to price in tighter monetary policy and generating negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show a positive or null correlation with CPIAUCSL increases across all rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly drop in real GDP exceeding 0.5% signals a demand shock that reduces corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of quarters where GDPC1 growth falls below -0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms broad-based labor market strength, supporting higher equity valuations and positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE fails to predict positive 20-day forward returns with a Rank IC above 0.04.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening phases, a sharp deceleration in industrial production reflects credit constraints on manufacturers, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO momentum during tightening cycles produces positive 5-day forward returns in over 60% of cases.
